title: Hinkin Law interview prep — Jan 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir prepping for an interview with Hinkin Law LLC (Jan 3 2025, post-China, pre-graduation).
  • Self-pitch positioning bullets:
    • Confident but not boastful.
    • Grants Pass roots — "have seen and volunteered at direct community levels."
    • Data-driven CS background.
    • AI-tools edge for social media — "Things that allow for multiple languages, AI captions workflows, long content into shorter more engaging videos."
  • Closing tactic: "Hinkin Law founder Joshua Hinkin has a book that I began reading in preparation for this interview." — Carnegie-style preparation in action (Dale Carnegie influence visible).

Key tactical move

"Hinkin Law founder Joshua Hinkin has a book that I began reading in preparation for this interview."

Why it matters in the graph

  • A transitional-job exploration in early 2025 — Yahir was clearly considering law-firm-marketing as a bridge-income path before committing to the Brotein / Austin route.
  • The AI-for-social-media pitch is a direct precursor to the ai search optimization / Rankett thesis nine months later — same competence sold to a different vertical.
  • "Grants Pass roots + community involvement" — Yahir using his hometown identity as a competitive moat for local-market marketing services.
  • Pairs with the 2024 07 04 questions mega notebook entry mentioning "call back Corvallis jobs" in the same general window.

Open questions

  • Did he get hired? Or did this redirect him to building Brotein/Rankett independently?
  • What's Joshua Hinkin's book called? Worth surfacing for the hinkin law entity stub.
